content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 10, 2006

Gary A. Taylor
9541 E. Marshall Street
Rosemead, California 91770

RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-06-156

Dear Mr. Taylor:

This letter is in response to your request regarding the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Your questions relate to activities
that occurred in 2005. The Commission does not advise with respect to past conduct.
(Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A), copy enclosed.) Therefore, nothing in this letter should be
construed to evaluate any conduct that may have already taken place. In addition,
because of the nature of your request, we will provide only informal assistance. Informal
assistance does not confer immunity under section 83114. (Regulation 18329(c)).²

QUESTIONS AND DISCUSSION

1. You have asked about the proper identification of a mailing sent to voters in
the City of Rosemead in 2005. Section 84305 provides:

"(a) Except as provided in subdivision (b), no candidate
or committee shall send a mass mailing unless the name, street
address, and city of the candidate or committee are shown on
the outside of each piece of mail in the mass mailing and on at
least one of the inserts included within each piece of mail of
the mailing in no less than 6-point type which shall be in a
color or print which contrasts with the background so as to be
easily legible. A post office box may be stated in lieu of a
street address if the organization's address is a matter of public
record with the Secretary of State.

___________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance may be provided where the advice or assistance sought relates to possible
amendments of previous reports filed by the person requesting the advice. (Regulation 18329(c)(4)(A).)

File No. 1-06-156
Page No. 2

(b) If the sender of the mass mailing is a single
candidate or committee, the name, street address, and city
of the candidate or committee need only be shown on the
outside of each piece of mail.

(c) If the sender of a mass mailing is a controlled
committee, the name of the person controlling the
committee shall be included in addition to the information
required by subdivision (a)."

A "mass mailing" is more than 200 pieces of substantially similar mail sent in a
calendar month. (Section 82041.5; regulation 18435.) The "sender" of a mass mailing is
the candidate or committee that pays for the largest portion of the design, printing, and
posting of the mailing. (Regulation 18435(b).)

2. Your second question is whether payments made by Rosemead P.R.I.D.E. for
the mailing resulted in a nonmonetary contribution to you. A "contribution" is made to
and received by a candidate or elected official if a payment is made for political purposes
and the payment is made "at the behest" of the affected candidate or elected official.
(Section 82015; regulation 18215.)

A payment is made "at the behest" of a candidate or elected official if it is made
under the control or at the direction of, in cooperation, consultation, coordination, or
concert with, at the request or suggestion of, or with the express, prior consent of the
candidate or elected official. (Regulation 18225.7.) If you were one of the elected
officials subject to a potential recall, payments for the cost of mailing the letter, which
was signed by you and asks voters not to sign recall petitions or to withdraw their petition
signatures, were contributions to you and you may need to amend your campaign
disclosure statements for 2005 to disclose them.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Carla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division

CW:rd
